REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF JERRY KU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED.PLAINTIFF JERRY KU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF JERRY H. KU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B)(". . .IF SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS SOUGHT, WHETHER SEPARATELY OR AS AN ALTERNATIVE TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE SPECIFIC CAUSE OF ACTION, AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, CLAIMS FOR DAMAGES, OR ISSUES OF DUTY MUST BE STATED SPECIFICALLY IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION AND BE REPEATED, VERBATIM, IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS"). PLAINTIFF JERRY H. KU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS MULTIPLE TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE ELEMENTS OF ADVERSE POSSESSION. THE DISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS INCLUDE, BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO: WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAD A GOOD FAITH BELIEF THAT HE WAS THE SOLE OWNER OF THE SUBJECT PROPERTY DURING THE REQUISITE TIME PERIOD DESPITE THE LETTER FROM ATTORNEY JOHN PRESTON EXPRESSLY ADVISING ON P.2, PARAGRAPH 2, THAT "ANY PROPERTY TRANSFERRED TO YOUR TRUST WILL BECOME COMMUNITY PROPERTY TO GIVE YOU AND YOUR SPOUSE THE INCOME TAX ADVANTAGES WHICH COMMUNITY PROPERTY HAS OVER JOINT TENANCY." (EXHBIT D TO KU DECLARATION.) SAID TRANSFER OF PLAINTIFF'S INTEREST FROM JOINT TENANCY TO COMMUNITY PROPERTY WAS SUBSEQUENTLY MADE (EXHIBIT C TO THE KU DECLARATION). THIS CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WITH RESPECT TO PLAINTIFF'S BELIEF OF SOLE OWNERSHIP OF THE PROPERTY. (JERRY KU DECLARATION 4.) THE COURT ALSO FINDS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF EVER ENGAGED IN BEHAVIOR THAT WAS SO OPEN, NOTORIOUS AND UNEQUIVOCAL IN CHARACTER THAT DEFENDANTS WERE GIVEN NOTICE OF INTENTION TO OUST HIS FAMILIAL CO-TENANTS. (SEE HACIENDA RANCH HOMES, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT, (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 1122,1128.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.131

Plaintiff Jerry H. Ku Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christian P. Foote, esq. (650-342-9600 / 650-696-2524) of Carr McClellan for Plaintiff/Moving party.
John S. Huster, esq. (415-265-0170) for Defendant/opposing party.

Plaintiff ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JERRY KU's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication is DENIED.

PLAINTIFF JERRY KU's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication is DENIED.

Plaintiff Jerry H. Ku's motion for summary adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b)(". . .If summary adjudication is sought, whether separately or as an alternative to the motion for summary judgment, the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts").

Plaintiff Jerry H. Ku's motion for summary judgement is DENIED. The court finds multiple triable issue of material fact as to the elements of adverse possession. The disputed material facts include, but are not limited to:
Whether Plaintiff had a good faith belief that he was the sole owner of the subject property during the requisite time period despite the letter from attorney John Preston expressly advising on p.2, paragraph 2, that "any property transferred to your Trust will become community property to give you and your spouse the income tax advantages which community property has over joint tenancy." (Exhbit D to Ku Declaration.) Said transfer of Plaintiff's interest from joint tenancy to community property was subsequently made (Exhibit C to the Ku Declaration). This creates a triable issue of fact with respect to Plaintiff's belief of sole ownership of the property. (Jerry Ku Declaration 4.) The Court also finds a triable issue of fact as to whether Plaintiff ever engaged in behavior that was so open, notorious and unequivocal in character that Defendants were given notice of intention to oust his familial co-tenants. (See Hacienda Ranch Homes, Inc. v. Superior Court, (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1122,1128.)

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Mar-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christian P. Foote, Esq., Carr McClellan, 650-342-9600, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The Court modifies it's tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JERRY KU'S Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendants Anna Ku Wong, Rena Ku Chin, Ava Ku, Beulah Ku, Harold Fong, Harry Fong And Melinda Low To Plaintiff Jerry H. Ku Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One.

The motion to compel is GRANTED IN PART - Defendants are ordered to produce a privilege log.

"The 'joint defense privilege' and the 'common interest privilege' have not been recognized by statute in California." OXY Resources Cal. LLC v. Super. Ct., 115 Cal. App. 4th 874, 889 (2004). "Therefore, a party seeking to rely on the common interest doctrine does not satisfy its burden to justify a claim of privilege simply by demonstrating that a confidential communication took place between parties who purportedly share a common interest. Rather, the party seeking to invoke the doctrine must first establish that the communicated information would otherwise be protected from disclosure by a claim of privilege." Id. at 890. "The next step in the analysis is to determine whether disclosing the information to a party outside the attorney-client relationship waived any applicable privileges." Id. ; see also Behunin v. Super. Ct., 9 Cal. App. 5th 833, 853 (2017) (discussing common interest doctrine).

A court may not order production of materials claimed to be privileged without permitting the opportunity to support the claim. Catalina Island Yacht Club v. Super. Ct., 242 Cal. App. 4th 1116, 1131 (2015). Because Defendants' objections are based on a claim of privilege, Defendants are required to provide a privilege log that will allow Plaintiff - and the Court if necessary - to determine whether the communications qualify for a privilege, and then whether any disclosures were made that would operate to waive privilege. "[A] privilege log typically should provide the identity and capacity of all individuals who authored, sent, or received each allegedly privileged document, the document's date, a brief description of the document and its contents or subject matter sufficient to determine whether the privilege applies, and the precise privilege or protection asserted." Id. at 1130; see also Code of Civ. Pro. 2031.240(b)(2), (c)(1).

Any materials that, on review by Defendants, do not qualify for privilege must be produced.

The request for sanctions was not made in the notice of motion and is therefore DENIED. Code of Civ. Pro. 2023.040. The previlege log to be produced on or before April 5, 2019.

Order is signed.
